rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALBION NIXON TRUST (*****COTTAGE GROVE REVOCABLE TRUST TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO*****)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners And William Kirbys Notice Of Petition And Petition For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/21/15. Endorsed filed courtesy paper copies of all filed documents requiring Court review was required to have been provided to the probate department not later than 1:30 p.m. of the day following electronic filing [Local Rule 2.10T]. A copy of the trust petition and supporting documents must be delivered to the probate examiners in room 202 at least 3 days prior to continued date, along with a proposed order. Notice of Hearing on proper Judicial Council Form needs to also be filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LUCY MAE GRIMES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Declare Jerome L. Grimes A Vexatious Litigant
rulings.Rulings: The motion to declare Jerome Grimes a vexatious litigant under CCP Sections 391 and 391.7 is granted. Mr. Grimes will be prohibited from filing any new pleadings without first obtaining leave of the presiding judge. The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. The pre-filing order shall use the mandatory judicial council form MC-700. The clerk will then provide a copy of the order to the Judicial Council. Any party wishing to contest the tentative must contact the court and opposing counsel no later than 4:00 p.m. on January 6, 2015.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MANFRED MOSES TESTAMENTARY TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Moving Party In Support Of Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Number One And Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: The unopposed motion to compel responses to request for production of documents, set number one is granted. Respondent must serve responses without objections no later than 15 days from this order. Failure to do so will result in the imposition of the full sanctions in the amount of $900.00 (without the need to file a motion) and may serve as the basis for evidentiary and/or terminating sanctions. Should Respondent fully comply with the Court's order, the sanctions will be reduced to $510 ($450 for filing the motion and $60 for the filing fee). The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the court's tentative must contact the court and opposing counsel no later than January 6, 2015 before 4:00 p.m.
